companyProfile.companyDescription: Tata Consultancy Services Ltd is an India-based company engaged in providing information technology (IT) services, consulting, and business solutions. Its business segments include Banking, Financial Services and Insurance; Manufacturing; Consumer Business; Communication, Media and Technology; Life Sciences and Healthcare and others, such as Energy, Resources and Utilities, s-Governance, and Products. The Company's products include TCS ADD, TCS BaNCS, TCS BFSI Platforms, TCS CHROMA, TCS Customer Intelligence & Insights, TCS ERP on Cloud, TCS Intelligent Urban Exchange, Quartz-The Smart Ledgers, Jile, TCS Optumera, TCS TwinX, TCS TAP and TCS OmniStore. Its services portfolio consists of Artificial Intelligence, Cloud, Cognitive Business Operations, Consulting, Cybersecurity, Data and Analytics, Enterprise Solutions, Internet of Things (IoT), and Digital Engineering, TCS Interactive, TCS and AWS Cloud, TCS Enterprise Cloud, TCS and Google Cloud, TCS and Microsoft Cloud and others.

recentNews.headline: India's mid-tier IT firms narrow new business gap with the larger rivals
recentNews.summary: Coforge, Mphasis and five similar companies saw growth moderate, but their decline in new business in FY26 was slower than that of the country’s six largest software exporters, which include the likes of Tata Consultancy Services (TCS) and Infosys.

recentNews.headline: TCS final dividend 2026: Tata Group IT major announces record date for ₹31 final payout
recentNews.summary: Tata Consultancy Services has set May 25 as the record date for a final dividend of ₹31 per share for FY26. Shareholders must own the stock before this date to qualify for the payout, which will be distributed on June 12, 2026.

recentNews.headline: Mcap of four of top-10 most valued firms surges by ₹2.20 lakh crore; Reliance biggest winner
recentNews.summary: The market valuation of leading firms surged by ₹2.20 lakh crore last week. Despite this, several banks experienced valuation declines due to rising crude oil prices and foreign investor outflows.

recentNews.headline: Indian IT’s big boys admit concerns around AI-led deflation

videoBody.body: <iframe width="560" height="315" src="https://www.youtube.com/embed/zyk3P7xBX8k" frameborder="0" allow="accelerometer; autoplay; clipboard-write; encrypted-media; gyroscope; picture-in-picture; web-share" referrerpolicy="strict-origin-when-cross-origin" allowfullscreen></iframe>

image.caption: The arrival of AI models like Anthropic's Mythos, which can detect and fix software bugs with minimal human intervention, has prompted analysts to question the efficiency of the IT services work that companies like TCS and Infosys do.

recentNews.summary: Over the last two weeks, bosses of Tata Consultancy Services Ltd, Infosys Ltd, HCL Technologies Ltd, Wipro Ltd, and Tech Mahindra Ltd have all pointed to AI squeezing revenues, and addressed concerns around higher productivity benefits.

recentNews.headline: Infosys has its best growth in 3 years, but Big Five have another uncertain year ahead
recentNews.summary: Much like Infosys, its Big Five peers Tata Consultancy Services, Wipro, HCL Technologies and Tech Mahindra have all voiced concerns about the troubled demand environment

recentNews.headline: TCS to ITC- Jay Thakkar suggests 3 stocks to buy or sell for short-term in F&O segment
recentNews.summary: The Indian stock market shows a negative start with Gift Nifty trading at 24,445. Despite a positive close on April 21, analysts highlight short covering and support levels at 24,500, with potential targets up to 25,200. Recommendations include buying futures for Bank of India, TCS, and ITC.

recentNews.headline: TCS Nashik case update: ‘Matter is sensitive, revealing,’ says fact-finding committee member amid ongoing probe
recentNews.summary: A fact-finding committee is investigating allegations of religious conversion and sexual harassment at Tata Consultancy Services' Nashik office. A panel member called the matter ‘sensitive’ and ‘revealing.’

recentNews.headline: Shocking allegations in TCS case emerge: From 'send your wife' to turning ‘Gulshan’— What we know
recentNews.summary: New allegations emerge in the investigation of claims of forced religious conversions and harassment at TCS's Nashik office. An employee claims he was told to ‘send his wife’ to an accused for children, while an old photo reportedly links an accused with a converted employee.

recentNews.headline: What Anthropic’s powerful new model means for Indian IT stocks
recentNews.summary: Infosys, Tata Consultancy Services (TCS), LTIMindtree, Coforge, Wipro, and L&T Technology Services are among the major IT players that have all fallen more than 20% on a year-to-date (YTD) basis.

recentNews.headline: TCS, Infosys, Wipro to HCL Technologies: IT stocks crash following sell-off in Indian stock market
recentNews.summary: HCL Technologies, Tata Consultancy Services (TCS), Persistent Systems, Infosys and Wipro were the top losers in the Nifty IT index, falling over 1% each. On the contrary, Coforge and Mphasis were the only gainers in the index.
